meta.description: deaconstruckdead graph — compiled from catalog, writings, and changelog

surface.preview: Original song by akabilly. Recorded as master version.
Original song by akabilly. Recorded as master version.
Original song by akabilly. Rough draft version of the foundational deaconstruckdead paradigm piece.

Pixel art collection exploring 8-bit aesthetics and retro digital imagery. Thirty distinct works examining low-resolution visual language, color constraint, and the expressive limits of pixel-based rendering. A systematic study in minimalist digital form.

Comprehensive digital glitch art collection exploring visual corruption, digital artifacts, and aesthetic decay. Features systematic variations and iterations exploring the boundaries between intention and error. An extensive study in digital entropy and visual disruption.
Original song by akabilly. Available in both MP3 and WAV formats.
Original song by akabilly. Mastered version available with Clear Sky mastering.
Original song by akabilly. Mastered version available with Aurora mastering.
Original song by akabilly. Available in both WAV and MP3 formats.
Original song by akabilly. Recorded as 32-bit float, 96kHz stereo master.
Mixed media painting — acrylic, oil pastels, chalk pastels, pens, pencils, markers on canvas. One of the foundational pieces in the akabilly catalog, paired with the song of the same name.
Original song by akabilly. Mastered version available with Aurora mastering.
Original song by akabilly. Available in both MP3 and WAV formats.
Original song by akabilly. Recorded as master version.
Original song by akabilly. Available in multiple versions including mix 2.
Original song by akabilly. Mastered version available with Aurora mastering.

Expansive video glitch art collection spanning motion-based digital corruption and temporal distortion. Features 142 unique pieces exploring glitch artifacts in video, frame disruption, color channel separation, and aesthetic breakdown through moving image. A systematic deconstruction of video stability and visual coherence.
